extraction.one_line_summary: GEOGHEGAN HART won Giro on final ITT by 39 seconds over HINDLEY after starting final stage level on time.
tactical_patterns: GEOGHEGAN HART pulled 30 seconds on HINDLEY by 10km checkpoint, revealing superior ITT pacing execution under pressure.
GANNA beat teammate DENNIS by 32 seconds despite DENNIS peaking for GC support, showing specialization hierarchy matters.
KELDERMAN moved onto podium via ITT from outside top-3, exposing softer competition when GC leaders neutralize each other.

rider_signals.GEOGHEGAN HART Tao: positive: Extracted 39-second margin in 15.7km ITT after starting dead-even, elite clutch TT performance under maximum pressure
rider_signals.HINDLEY Jai: negative: Lost 30 seconds by 10km checkpoint in must-win ITT, suggesting pacing collapse or insufficient TT-specific preparation
rider_signals.GANNA Filippo: positive: Beat CAMPENAERTS by 32 seconds for fourth stage win, but DENNIS finishing second shows domestique role limited peak form
rider_signals.DENNIS Rohan: positive: Second on stage after mountain domestique duties suggests maintained ITT form despite GC support workload
rider_signals.KELDERMAN Wilco: positive: Moved onto final podium via ITT while GC leaders battled suggests underrated time trialist when fresh
rider_signals.ALMEIDA João: positive: Moved from fifth to fourth on final ITT, recovered form after mid-race decline
